updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.212: /fork Redesign, WebSearch & Subagent Session Caps, MCP Auto-Background
updates.summary: Anthropic released Claude Code v2.1.212 on July 17, introducing a redesigned /fork command that now copies the current conversation to a new background session instead of spawning an in-session subagent. The update also adds session-wide safety limits: WebSearch calls are capped at 200 per session and subagent spawns at 200 per session, and MCP tool calls running longer than 2 minutes are automatically backgrounded. A new /resume picker lets users select and return to any past session, and the claude auto-mode reset command was added.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog, which mirrors docs.anthropic.com/en/release-notes/claude-code. Version number and date confirmed via multiple third-party changelog aggregators. All feature details match the official changelog entry. No red flags.

Slide 4: Subagent spawns also capped at 200 — agentic loops stay in check

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.214: Permission Security Patches, EndConversation Tool, OpenTelemetry & Memory Improvements
updates.summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Code v2.1.214 today (July 18), patching multiple permission-system security vulnerabilities: a bypass in single-segment dir/** allow rules that was auto-approving writes, a permission-check bypass in Windows PowerShell 5.1, and a bypass for Bash commands longer than 10,000 characters. The release also adds an EndConversation tool enabling Claude Code to gracefully terminate sessions involving abusive users, a progress heartbeat for long-running tool calls, ISO-formatted modified timestamps in memory file frontmatter, and new OpenTelemetry attributes (message.uuid, client_request_id, tool_source) for observability.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the Claude Code changelog. Version number and date confirmed via the changelog aggregator at code.claude.com. Security fixes are described at the changelog level (no CVE IDs assigned). The EndConversation tool is a notable product addition with policy implications. No red flags.

slides: Slide 1: Claude Code v2.1.214 — Security Hardening (July 18, 2026)
Slide 2: Patched: dir/** allow rules were silently approving writes
Slide 3: Patched: Windows PowerShell 5.1 permission bypass
Slide 4: Patched: Long Bash commands (>10,000 chars) bypassed permission checks
Slide 5: New: EndConversation tool — Claude can now exit abusive sessions

linkedin.angle: Enterprise security and responsible AI deployment — today's Claude Code security patch is a reminder that permission models in agentic tools need continuous hardening
